title: Today's AI Brief: Anthropic's Cowork and Google's Scam-Fighting Phone
summary: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent for non-tech users, and Google's Phone app gets AI-powered scam detection.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
imageCaption: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
imageSourceTitle: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. I've got two fresh stories to share with you today. First up, Anthropic has just launched Cowork, a new AI agent that brings the power of its Claude Code tool to non-technical users. No coding required, just a simple desktop agent that works right in your files.

Anthropic's Cowork is essentially a desktop agent that lets you interact with your files using natural language. Imagine being able to ask questions or make requests about your documents without having to write a single line of code. That's what Cowork offers, and it's pretty cool. According to the folks at Anthropic, the team built this feature with the goal of making AI more accessible to a wider range of users.

Next, Google's Phone app is getting a scam-fighting boost. The app will now flag suspicious calls that might be impersonating one of your contacts. It's like having a personal assistant who helps you screen unwanted calls. This feature uses AI to detect potential scams, and it's a nice example of how technology can help keep us safe online.

items: Anthropic's Cowork aims to bring AI to non-technical users, making it easier to work with files and documents.
Google's Phone app uses AI to detect and flag suspicious calls that might be scams.

body.text: My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.

sources.title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
Google’s Phone app will tell you if a scammer is impersonating one of your contacts
